title: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease + ADA Standards of Care
source: Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) / American Diabetes Association (ADA)
description: Comprehensive guidelines for management of type 2 diabetes in patients with chronic kidney disease, including medication safety thresholds, cardiorenal protection, glycemic targets, and management of AKI superimposed on CKD.
recommendations: METFORMIN CONTRAINDICATION: Metformin is CONTRAINDICATED when eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m² due to risk of lactic acidosis — DISCONTINUE IMMEDIATELY
METFORMIN DOSE REDUCTION: Reduce metformin dose by 50% when eGFR 30-45; reassess every 3 months
METFORMIN AND AKI: Hold metformin during any acute illness or procedure that may cause acute kidney injury
PREFERRED AGENT CKD+T2DM: SGLT2 inhibitor (empagliflozin, dapagliflozin) with eGFR >=20 for cardiorenal protection — first-line after metformin discontinuation
GLP-1 RECEPTOR AGONIST: Semaglutide or liraglutide appropriate for CKD stage 3-4 with cardiovascular disease
GLYCEMIC TARGET CKD: HbA1c target 7-8% for CKD stage 4-5 to minimize hypoglycemia risk
SULFONYLUREAS: Use with extreme caution in CKD — glipizide preferred over glyburide; reduce dose by 50% for eGFR <30; HIGH hypoglycemia risk
HYPERKALEMIA MANAGEMENT: Monitor potassium closely; ACE inhibitor/ARB may worsen hyperkalemia in CKD stage 4-5
FLUID MANAGEMENT AKI-on-CKD: Restrict sodium to <2g/day; restrict fluids if oliguric; furosemide for hypervolemia
SULFONAMIDE ALLERGY AND FUROSEMIDE: Furosemide contains sulfonamide moiety — theoretical cross-reactivity with sulfonamide allergy; use bumetanide or ethacrynic acid as alternative if allergy severe
BLOOD PRESSURE TARGET: <130/80 mmHg for CKD with diabetes; ACE inhibitor or ARB first-line for proteinuria
AVOID NEPHROTOXINS: NSAIDs, IV contrast, aminoglycosides — contraindicated or use with extreme caution in CKD stage 4
DIALYSIS PREPARATION: Begin dialysis education for CKD stage 4 (eGFR 15-29); consider AV fistula placement
AKI REVERSAL: Identify and treat reversible causes — obstruction, contrast nephropathy, volume depletion, nephrotoxins
